title: Crepidomanes insigne
taxonomy: Crepidomanes insigne is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae, commonly known as the filmy ferns. This plant is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the Old World. As a member of the Hymenophyllaceae family, it is classified within the kingdom Plantae, division Pteridophyta, and class Polypodiopsida. The genus Crepidomanes is a group of ferns that are primarily epiphytic and have delicate, filmy fronds. Crepidomanes insigne is a distinctive species within this genus, characterized by its unique frond shape and size.

intro: Crepidomanes insigne, also known as the "Signate Crepidomanes," is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Crepidomanes insigne include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
